title: Wholesome Filipino Yautia Stew
description: A resinous, creamy stew that blends Filipino flavors with a shio-koji cure, using yautia root and hearty protein sources, perfect for a quick weeknight dinner.

ingredients.name: chicken breast
ingredients.notes: sliced thin and marinated in shio‑koji for 15 min

instructions: Marinate sliced chicken breast in 1 tablespoon of shio‑koji for 15 minutes.
Heat 2 teaspoons of vegetable oil in a kadhai over medium heat and sauté minced garlic, julienned ginger, and chopped onion until fragrant.
Add the marinated chicken to the kadhai and cook until it turns opaque, about 5 minutes.
Stir in the cubed yautia root and cook for an additional 2 minutes, allowing the flavors to mingle.
Pour in the coconut milk and coconut cream, then add soy sauce, salt, and black pepper; bring to a gentle simmer.
Reduce the heat to low, cover the kadhai, and let the stew cook for 15 minutes, or until the yautia is tender and the sauce has thickened.
Taste and adjust seasoning if needed, then remove from heat.
Serve the stew hot, optionally garnished with fresh cilantro or sliced scallions.
